narrative: Jindal Stainless was the overall FY2024-25 leader. It ranked 1st on employee LTIFR, worker LTIFR, employee TRIFR, worker TRIFR, employee fatalities, worker fatalities, affected employees and affected workers. Its zero LTIFR for employees/workers, zero employee/worker fatalities, and zero affected employees/workers indicate the strongest disclosed safety outcome profile. SAIL was generally second-best on worker TRIFR and affected workers but continued to report employee and worker fatalities. JSW was competitive on fatalities but worsened in worker TRIFR and affected workers. Tata’s FY25 picture is mixed: LTIFR improved, worker fatalities declined, and rehabilitation support was strongest, but Tata ranked 4th on both LTIFR categories, both TRIFR categories and affected employees/workers. Tata’s FY25 safety programme is directionally robust: ISO 45001-based EHSMS, fatality risk control, contractor safety audits, road/rail safety controls, process safety management and EnsafeNxt-based incident learning. The priority is converting these controls into reduced recordable and high-consequence worker outcomes.
